edges.description: Fibrous bands can entangle fetal parts and act as circumferential constricting structures.
Constriction bands can compress distal tissues and impair perfusion or neural integrity.
Mechanical entanglement can disrupt, deform, or malform fetal structures that otherwise developed normally.
Tissue compression and perfusion compromise contribute to structural fetal disruption and deformation.

nodes.description: Lower-limb abnormalities are frequent in Orphanet.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002814 | Abnormality of the lower limb | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Upper-limb abnormalities are occasional in Orphanet.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002817 | Abnormality of the upper limb | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Amniotic constriction rings are a frequent defining manifestation in Orphanet.
Cleft lip is an occasional craniofacial manifestation in Orphanet.
Cleft palate is an occasional craniofacial manifestation in Orphanet.

ABS abnormalities arise through disruptive, deformational, and malformational effects on fetal organs and structures, producing a wide spectrum from isolated constriction rings to severe limb, neural-tube, craniofacial, and body-wall anomalies.

evidence.snippet: congenital anomalies by disruption, deformation, and malformation of organs

evidence.reference_title: Revisiting amniotic band sequence: a wide spectrum of manifestations.
evidence.snippet: ABS is a polymorphous syndrome and recognizing its various manifestations can be of help in prenatal identification.

evidence.reference_title: Amniotic band syndrome and limb body wall complex in Europe 1980-2019.
evidence.snippet: Limb anomalies and neural tube defects were commonly seen in ABS

nodes.description: Circumferential compression can cause edema, reduced distal limb perfusion, neural compromise, umbilical cord involvement, tissue loss, and fetal functional impairment.

evidence.snippet: Compression from the rings may lead to edema, skeletal anomalies (e.g. fractures, foot deformities) and, infrequently, neural compromise.

evidence.reference_title: An update on amniotic bands sequence.
evidence.snippet: release of the amniotic bands using fetoscopy with recovery of the involved distal limb perfusion

evidence.reference_title: Fetoscopic approach to amniotic band syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Four of five patients were found to have involvement of the umbilical cord at the time of fetoscopy.

nodes.description: Fetal parts can become entrapped by amniotic bands, producing circumferential rings or bands on limbs and other body regions. This mechanical process is the proximal abnormality that produces the sequence of disruption and deformation.

evidence.snippet: fetal parts becoming entangled in the amnion.
partial to complete, congenital, fibrous, circumferential, constriction bands/rings on any part of the body

nodes.description: Selected prenatal cases with threatened limb or cord compromise may undergo fetoscopic division or release of amniotic bands to prevent amputation, dysfunction, or fetal demise, while balancing risks of membrane rupture and preterm birth.

evidence.reference_title: Fetoscopic Release of Amniotic Band Syndrome: An Update.
evidence.snippet: fetoscopic release of the amniotic bands in case of amniotic band syndrome is feasible with encouraging results in order to prevent amputation and dysfunction of the extremities.

evidence.reference_title: Fetoscopic surgery for amniotic band syndrome: Case series.
evidence.snippet: The majority of the children acquired a functional limb (71.4%).

evidence.reference_title: Fetoscopic approach to amniotic band syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Fetoscopic amniotic band release is safe.

nodes.description: Amniotic band syndrome involves fetal-placental or amniotic fibrous bands in the amniotic cavity. The causal pathway is heterogeneous and likely multifactorial rather than explained by one mechanism in every case.

evidence.reference_title: An update on amniotic bands sequence.
evidence.snippet: may be associated with fetalplacental fibrous bands.

evidence.reference_title: An update on amniotic bands sequence.
evidence.snippet: Different theories have attempted to explain the etiology of amniotic band sequence; however, none has individually been able to support each and every defect observed

nodes.description: Postnatal surgery may be used for constriction-ring release, reconstruction, cleft or body-wall repair, or treatment of limb deformity when fetal intervention is not indicated or no longer possible.

evidence.reference_title: An update on amniotic bands sequence.
evidence.snippet: the possibility of performing a post-natal surgical repair.
